last_verdict.tests: 1958 passed, 25 skipped (.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests -q). FIVE waves + a LIVE test this session. Wave 5: LIVE-validated asr-tts-2 (async second pass ENGAGED, 0 underflows/exceptions/crashes/self-interrupts, barge-in cuts real talk-overs) BUT surfaced two problems: (1) the MIC IS CLIPPING 36-65% of blocks (peak=1.0, rms~0.5) -> OS mic gain too hot -> garbled STT + SenseVoice hallucinations ('I.'); OWNER must lower OS mic input level/disable mic-boost (input_gain=1.0 so it's not code). (2) owner CONFIRMED real acoustic OUTPUT artifacts (buzzy/glitchy TTS, not just garbled words) = the run-103630 white-noise still partly present; _audio_cb silently zero-fills FIFO underruns. ADDED DIAGNOSTICS (real-time-safe, reviewed SHIP): input-clipping warning in the capture heartbeat (clip=% + one-shot WARN >2%), output-underrun counter in _audio_cb (per-reply WARN + playback_underrun metric + heartbeat underruns=N). NEXT: owner lowers mic gain + re-runs -> clip% should ~0 (STT jumps) and underruns=N confirms whether the buzzy artifacts are FIFO underruns from CPU contention (async SenseVoice worker + TTS synth on the desktop CPU tier) -> if so, per-tier TTS/worker thread budgeting. Also: auto-detect picked 'desktop' not 'desktop_gpu_4090' (probe reads 16376MB < 16GB threshold -- recommend_profile off-by-rounding). --- Earlier waves 1-4: Wave 4 (low-value headless cleanup): llm-inference-9 (LlamaCppLLM type_k/type_v KV-cache quant plumbing; phone/phone_lite quantize the K cache to q8_0, V left at f16 since V-quant needs flash-attn; fail-soft on an old lib) + control-plane-2 (load-elastic admission: supervisor caps concurrency to 1 under sustained load via an ungated load_fraction reader threaded app->runtime->supervisor, separate from the live_routing-gated load_snapshot). DEFERRED with reasons: llm-inference-6 (per-call timeout blocked by the single-context lock; supervisor 25s reap backstops it), llm-inference-7 (Q3 weights config flip needs on-device quality validation). Reviewed (1 refuted, nits+1 LOW; LOW fixed -> K-only). --- Wave 3: Wave 3 (headless): control-plane-3 -- EWMA-scaled ADAPTIVE watchdog deadlines. The recorder now folds a TTS first-audio EWMA (recent_tts_ms) alongside the TTFT one; the watchdog's 'llm stuck'/'tts stuck' deadlines = clamp(4*recent_ewma, floor, ceil) with cold-start fallback to the static 10s/5s. LLM floor 6s (above a heavy-tier first-token, since the EWMA blends tiers), ceil 30s; ADAPTIVE_DEADLINES toggle pins legacy. Net: fast device surfaces a real stall sooner, slow phone stops false-flagging honest long turns (attacks the stuck:1 bundle noise). Diagnosis only -- supervisor task-reap is the real hang bound. Adversarially reviewed (0 refuted, nits+1 LOW, fixed). Scouted first: routing-cascade-6 already neutralized by LLMCapabilityRouter cache, routing-cascade-7 done, routing-cascade-4/asr-tts-3 need real models/audio. --- Wave 1+2: Wave 1: asr-tts-2 (async second-pass ASR) + asr-tts-1 (per-tier ASR policy). Wave 2 (headless, no live test): llm-inference-3 (LlamaCppLLM num_predict->max_tokens cap translation + phone/phone_lite caps) + rc-5 (SUPERSEDED metric so newest-input-wins-preempted turns don't false-flag 'llm stuck') + invariant hardening (no profile may disable speaker_gate_input / agent_brain.require_owner_verified). Both waves adversarially reviewed; wave-2 review CAUGHT A BLOCKER (a config _comment key leaking into llama.cpp's strict create_chat_completion signature) -> fixed by dropping _-prefixed option keys + a strict-signature regression test. Scouted Phase-1 first: device-adapt-1/cross-platform-8/cloud-invariant were already done.
last_verdict.current: 1992 passed, 24 skipped. 2026-06-19: autonomous harness RUN ON THE OWNER'S REAL RECORDED VOICE. Guided recorder (tools.autotest record) shipped + a record --check mic METER. BIG FIX: the built-in mic was clipping EVERYTHING (peak 1.0, 5-37% of samples) -> garbled STT. Root cause: built-in mic analog gain MAXED (Capture +30dB AND Internal Mic Boost +30dB = +60dB). The ALSA mixer can't be tamed (PipeWire manages/overrides it; parecord/pw-record IGNORE software volume) -- the knob that STICKS and that the sounddevice recorder HONORS is the PipeWire SOURCE VOLUME: `pactl set-source-volume @DEFAULT_SOURCE@ 40%` (persists). At 40% the owner records clean (clip 0%, rms ~0.07-0.14). Gotchas: the mic SOURCE SUSPENDS when idle -> one-shot sounddevice capture reads digital silence (hold awake with a throwaway `parecord ... &`; a full recording self-holds). CABLE RESULT on the owner's clean voice: mean WER 0.29 across 37 clips, 0 dropped, ALL 5 commands perfect, self-corrections UNDERSTOOD ('call John no James'->James; timer->10min; repeats collapsed). Needed a harness fix: CableAcoustics.inject_gain 100->55 (the engine captures the null-sink monitor ~2.7x hot -> unity injection clipped it -> ratcheted the echo/quiet floor -> dropped every clip after ~14 on LONG runs; short runs hid it). Remaining cable artifact: engine clips the FIRST PHONEME of each injected clip ('St an alarm','G me a recipe') -> add ~0.3s lead-in silence to injections -> expect WER ~0.18 (NOT done). OTA (over-the-air) WIRED but NOT yet working: added SpeakerAcoustics(inject_sink=) + --inject-sink so user clips play out a DIFFERENT speaker than the assistant (near/far). Owner added a JBL Flip 5 (bluez_output.D8_37_3B_19_CF_03.1, default sink = assistant output; A2DP output-only so laptop mic still captures). A 6-clip OTA subset FAILED (monitor_rms 0.059, STT garbage): mic at 40% + laptop speaker sink at 35% = user-clip playback too quiet at the mic. NEEDS LEVEL TUNING (raise laptop speaker + mic for OTA). OTA re-captures recordings through a speaker -> degraded STT (judge STT from CABLE, not OTA); OTA's value = self-interrupt/barge under the real JBL echo (JBL is BT -> ~150-250ms extra latency, so real ref delay >> configured 260ms; aec_probe the bundle). Owner asked to STOP live tuning + prepare setup for next session. Handoff: docs/session_2026-06-19_real_voice_ota_setup.md. recordings/ + logs/runs/ bundles hold the owner's REAL VOICE -> kept LOCAL ONLY (§9.7), not committed.

next: 2026-06-19 NEXT (owner runs next session; setup is PREPARED): OVER-THE-AIR test with the JBL. (1) Raise levels: `pactl set-sink-volume alsa_output.pci-0000_00_1f.3.analog-stereo 85%` (laptop speaker) + `pactl set-source-volume @DEFAULT_SOURCE@ 70%` (mic FOR OTA; set BACK to 40% before any direct-voice re-record). (2) Run a small OTA subset, confirm monitor_rms ~0.2-0.3 + clips get recognized -> assistant speaks. (3) Full OTA: `.venv/bin/python -m tools.autotest voice --acoustics speaker --make-sound --inject-sink alsa_output.pci-0000_00_1f.3.analog-stereo --utterances recordings/owner --llm ollama --model gemma3:4b`. (4) `python -m tools.aec_probe logs/runs/run-<id>.wav --backend dtln --max-delay-ms 800` on the OTA bundle -> set aec_ref_delay_ms to the real JBL+acoustic delay. OPTIONAL CABLE STT WIN: add ~0.3s lead-in silence to injected clips (fixes first-phoneme drop) -> WER ~0.18. Devices: JBL=bluez_output.D8_37_3B_19_CF_03.1 (default sink=assistant); laptop speaker=alsa_output.pci-0000_00_1f.3.analog-stereo (inject user voice, near mic); mic=alsa_input.pci-0000_00_1f.3.analog-stereo. ENV: use .venv/bin/python; mic source volume now 40% (the clipping fix). Full detail: docs/session_2026-06-19_real_voice_ota_setup.md.
